<type>file</type>
<content>
1: ---
2: title: deep-research
3: description: Autonomous deep research loop with lineage-aware lifecycle branches, reducer-owned packet synchronization, and runtime parity guidance.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "deep research loop"
6:   - "autonomous iterative research"
7:   - "lineage aware research"
8:   - "fresh context research"
9:   - "reducer synchronized research packet"
10: ---
11: 
12: # deep-research
13: 
14: > Autonomous multi-iteration research loop for topics that need repeated investigation, fresh context per iteration, and durable packet state on disk.
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: <!-- ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
19: ## TABLE OF CONTENTS
20: 
21: 1. [OVERVIEW](#1--overview)
22: 2. [QUICK START](#2--quick-start)
23: 3. [FEATURES](#3--features)
24: 4. [STRUCTURE](#4--structure)
25: 5. [CONFIGURATION](#5--configuration)
26: 6. [USAGE EXAMPLES](#6--usage-examples)
27: 7. [LIFECYCLE MODES](#7--lifecycle-modes)
28: 8. [RUNTIME PARITY](#8--runtime-parity)
29: 9. [TROUBLESHOOTING](#9--troubleshooting)
30: 10. [FAQ](#10--faq)
31: 11. [RELATED DOCUMENTS](#11--related-documents)
32: <!-- /ANCHOR:table-of-contents -->
33: 
34: ---
35: 
36: <!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
37: ## 1. OVERVIEW
38: 
39: ### Purpose
40: 
41: `deep-research` is research-only. It runs repeated investigation cycles through `/deep:start-research-loop`, dispatching a fresh `@deep-research` agent for each iteration while keeping continuity in packet files instead of live conversation memory.
42: 
43: ### Usage
44: 
45: Use this README for human orientation: command examples, packet layout, lifecycle modes, troubleshooting, and reference navigation. Use `SKILL.md` for runtime instructions and smart routing.
46: 
47: ### Key Statistics
48: 
49: | Metric | Value |
50: |---|---|
51: | Version | `1.13.0.0` |
52: | Primary command | `/deep:start-research-loop` |
53: | Runtime modes | `auto`, `confirm` |
54: | Live lifecycle branches | `new`, `resume`, `restart` |
55: | Focused references | 13 |
56: | Scripts | 2 primary helpers |
57: 
58: ### How This Compares
59: 
60: | Capability | This Skill | Related Skill |
61: |---|---|---|
62: | Research loop | Multi-round investigation and synthesis | `@context` handles single-pass lookup |
63: | Review loop | Out of scope | `deep-review` owns iterative code review |
64: | Shared runtime | Consumes executor and graph support | `deep-loop-runtime` owns shared libraries |
65: | Packet docs | Writes research artifacts under a spec folder | `system-spec-kit` owns spec validation and memory continuity |
66: 
67: ### Key Features
68: 
69: | Feature | What It Does |
70: |---|---|
71: | Fresh-context iteration | Starts each research pass with externalized packet state |
72: | Reducer synchronization | Keeps strategy, registry, dashboard, and synthesis metadata aligned |
73: | Legal-stop convergence | Blocks premature STOP until coverage and quality gates pass |
74: | Graph-aware gates | Uses `graphEvents` as extra STOP-blocking evidence when present |
75: | Spec anchoring | Bounded `spec.md` seed and generated-fence write-back during the workflow |
76: | Shared resource family | Quick reference, loop protocol, split convergence/state references, config, strategy, dashboard, prompt-pack, and runtime capability assets aligned with sibling deep skills while staying research-specific. |
77: 
78: The packet is lineage-aware. Every run carries `sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `generation`, and `continuedFromRun`, so the workflow can distinguish an active resume from a restart. `fork` and `completed-continue` are reserved for a future release and are not runtime-supported today. See the Lifecycle Branches section in `references/loop_protocol.md` for the canonical one-session contract.
79: 
80: The packet is also reducer-synchronized. The agent writes the iteration file plus the JSONL record. The workflow reducer then updates the machine-owned packet surfaces so `deep-research-strategy.md`, `findings-registry.json`, `deep-research-dashboard.md`, and synthesis metadata cannot drift apart.
81: 
82: The workflow also anchors every run to a real `spec.md`. During late INIT it follows [`references/spec_check_protocol.md`](references/spec_check_protocol.md): acquire `research/.deep-research.lock`, classify `folder_state` as `no-spec`, `spec-present`, `spec-just-created-by-this-run`, or `conflict-detected`, and then either seed a Level 1 spec or append bounded context before LOOP starts.
83: 
84: During SYNTHESIS the same contract replaces exactly one `<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: deep-research/spec-findings -->` ... `<!-- END GENERATED: deep-research/spec-findings -->` block under the chosen host anchor while keeping `research/research.md` canonical.
85: 
86: Outside the research loop itself, `/speckit:resume` remains the canonical recovery surface for packet work. Continuity still rebuilds from `handover.md`, then `_memory.continuity`, then the remaining spec docs, while generated memory artifacts stay supporting only.
87: 
88: For iterative code review, use `deep-review`.
89: <!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
90: 
91: ---
92: 
93: <!-- ANCHOR:quick-start -->
94: ## 2. QUICK START
95: 
96: ```bash
97: /deep:start-research-loop:auto "WebSocket reconnection strategies across browsers"
98: /deep:start-research-loop:confirm "Distributed cache invalidation patterns"
99: /deep:start-research-loop:auto "API backpressure patterns" --max-iterations 6 --convergence 0.03
100: ```
101: 
102: What the workflow creates under the resolved `{artifact_dir}`:
103: 
104: - `deep-research-config.json`
105: - `deep-research-state.jsonl`
106: - `deep-research-strategy.md`
107: - `findings-registry.json`
108: - `deep-research-dashboard.md`
109: - `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`
110: - `research.md`
111: 
112: The artifact directory always lives under the target spec's local `research/` folder. First runs with an empty local folder use `{spec_folder}/research/` directly, including child-phase and sub-phase targets. A `{spec_folder}/research/{ownerSlug}-pt-NN/` packet is allocated only when prior local content already exists for a different target. Pause a running loop by creating `.deep-research-pause` inside the resolved `{artifact_dir}`, then delete it to let the workflow continue from the next lifecycle check.
113: <!-- /ANCHOR:quick-start -->
114: 
115: ---
116: 
117: <!-- ANCHOR:features -->
118: ## 3. FEATURES
119: 
120: - Fresh context per iteration: Each iteration uses a fresh LEAF agent dispatch.
121: - Lineage-aware lifecycle: Supports `new`, `resume`, and `restart`. `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred. See the Lifecycle Branches section in `references/loop_protocol.md`.
122: - Reducer synchronization: Strategy, dashboard, registry, and synthesis metadata are updated from canonical iteration outputs.
123: - Packet-first recovery: Hook and non-hook runtimes derive the same next action from packet files.
124: - Runtime capability matrix: One documented and machine-readable source of truth for provider quirks and parity expectations.
125: - `spec.md` anchoring: Late INIT follows `references/spec_check_protocol.md` to seed or sync bounded packet context before LOOP begins.
126: - Folder-state contract: `folder_state` resolves to `no-spec`, `spec-present`, `spec-just-created-by-this-run`, or `conflict-detected` before any `spec.md` mutation branch runs.
127: - Advisory lock + generated fence: The workflow holds `research/.deep-research.lock` through synthesis and replaces one `BEGIN/END GENERATED` findings block in `spec.md`.
128: - Progressive synthesis: `research.md` can be updated incrementally and is finalized during synthesis.
129: - Negative knowledge: Ruled-out directions and dead ends are preserved as first-class outputs.
130: - Quality guards: Source diversity, focus alignment, and weak-source checks must pass before STOP is accepted.
131: - 3-signal convergence model: Composite stop decision uses Rolling Average (`0.30`), MAD Noise Floor (`0.35`), and Question Entropy (`0.35`). STOP requires a normalized weighted score above `0.60`, then legal-stop gates must pass.
132: - Graph-aware legal-stop checks: When `graphEvents` exist, structural graph signals add extra STOP-blocking evidence on top of the standard convergence math.
133: - Semantic coverage graph: Each iteration emits `graphEvents` (nodes + edges) in JSONL, building an in-memory coverage graph with relation types (ANSWERS, SUPPORTS, CONTRADICTS, SUPERSEDES, DERIVED_FROM, COVERS, CITES).
134: - Graph convergence guards: STOP-blocking guards. sourceDiversity (>= 0.4) and evidenceDepth (>= 1.5) must pass before convergence is accepted, preventing premature termination from single-source or shallow-evidence research.
135: - Question coverage tracking: Graph tracks which research questions have ANSWERS edges, computing an answerCoverage ratio that contributes to the convergence score.
136: - Fail-closed corruption handling: The reducer throws a structured error before writing any derived files when JSONL corruption is detected in non-lenient mode.
137: - Graph convergence fallback: When `blendedScore` is absent from `graph_convergence` events, the reducer uses a numeric fallback instead of collapsing to zero.
138: - Terminal stop metadata: The reducer parses `synthesis_complete` events to derive authoritative dashboard status rather than relying on stale config.
139: <!-- /ANCHOR:features -->
140: 
141: ---
142: 
143: <!-- ANCHOR:structure -->
144: ## 4. STRUCTURE
145: 
146: ```text
147: .opencode/skills/deep-research/
148:   SKILL.md
149:   README.md
150:   references/
151:     capability_matrix.md
152:     convergence.md
153:     convergence_graph.md
154:     convergence_recovery.md
155:     convergence_reference_only.md
156:     convergence_signals.md
157:     loop_protocol.md
158:     quick_reference.md
159:     spec_check_protocol.md
160:     state_format.md
161:     state_jsonl.md
162:     state_outputs.md
163:     state_reducer_registry.md
164:   assets/
165:     deep_research_config.json
166:     deep_research_dashboard.md
167:     deep_research_strategy.md
168:     prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl
169:     runtime_capabilities.json
170:   feature_catalog/
171:     feature_catalog.md
172:     01--loop-lifecycle/
173:     02--state-management/
174:     03--convergence/
175:     04--research-output/
176: ```
177: 
178: Runtime packet layout:
179: 
180: ```text
181: {spec_folder}/research/
182:   deep-research-config.json
183:   deep-research-state.jsonl
184:   deep-research-strategy.md
185:   findings-registry.json
186:   deep-research-dashboard.md
187:   .deep-research-pause
188:   research.md
189:   archive/
190:     {sessionId}/
191:   iterations/
192:     iteration-001.md
193:     iteration-002.md
194:   {ownerSlug}-pt-NN/                 # Conditional: prior non-matching content exists
195: ```
196: 
197: Ownership model:
198: 
199: - Agent-owned writes: `iteration-NNN.md`, JSONL iteration/event append, optional progressive synthesis content.
200: - Reducer-owned writes: `deep-research-strategy.md` machine-owned sections, `findings-registry.json`, `deep-research-dashboard.md`.
201: - Workflow-owned output: `research.md` and lifecycle snapshot files such as `synthesis-v{generation}.md`.
202: - Protocol-owned packet mutations: bounded `spec.md` seeding, context append, advisory lock lifecycle, and generated-fence replacement defined in `references/spec_check_protocol.md`.
203: <!-- /ANCHOR:structure -->
204: 
205: ---
206: 
207: <!-- ANCHOR:configuration -->
208: ## 5. CONFIGURATION
209: 
210: | Setting | Default | Purpose |
211: |---|---|---|
212: | `--max-iterations` | `10` | Hard iteration ceiling |
213: | `--convergence` | `0.05` | Novelty threshold for rolling convergence |
214: | `progressiveSynthesis` | `true` | Allows `research.md` updates before final synthesis |
215: | `stuckThreshold` | `3` | Consecutive no-progress iterations before recovery |
216: | `executor.kind` | `native` | Selects native LEAF agent or workflow-routed CLI executor |
217: 
218: Non-configurable invariants: the command YAML owns dispatch, `reduce-state.cjs` owns reducer writes, and `@deep-research` remains LEAF-only.
219: <!-- /ANCHOR:configuration -->
220: 
221: ---
222: 
223: <!-- ANCHOR:usage-examples -->
224: ## 6. USAGE EXAMPLES
225: 
226: **Autonomous investigation**
227: 
228: ```text
229: User request: Deep research browser WebSocket reconnection behavior
230: Skill routing: LOOP_SETUP + CONVERGENCE
231: Resources loaded: quick_reference, loop_protocol, convergence, state_format
232: Expected output: research packet with iterations, dashboard, registry, and synthesis
233: ```
234: 
235: **Resume an active packet**
236: 
237: ```text
238: User request: Continue the deep research run
239: Skill routing: STATE + ITERATION
240: Resources loaded: state_jsonl, state_outputs, state_reducer_registry, loop_protocol
241: Expected output: resumed lineage event and next iteration or synthesis
242: ```
243: 
244: **Diagnose a blocked STOP**
245: 
246: ```text
247: User request: Why did convergence not stop?
248: Skill routing: CONVERGENCE + RECOVERY
249: Resources loaded: convergence, convergence_signals, convergence_recovery, convergence_graph
250: Expected output: gate/blocker explanation and next focus
251: ```
252: <!-- /ANCHOR:usage-examples -->
253: 
254: ---
255: 
256: <!-- ANCHOR:lifecycle-modes -->
257: ## 7. LIFECYCLE MODES
258: 
259: | Mode | Meaning |
260: |------|---------|
261: | `new` | First run against this spec folder. No prior state. |
262: | `resume` | Continue the active lineage with the same `sessionId`. Persisted as a `resumed` JSONL event. |
263: | `restart` | Start a new generation with explicit parent linkage and archive the prior `research/` tree under `research_archive/{timestamp}/`. Persisted as a `restarted` JSONL event. |
264: | `fork` (deferred) | Reserved. Earlier drafts described this as a sibling-lineage branch. the runtime does not emit lineage events for `fork` today. Do not expose it in user-facing workflows. |
265: | `completed-continue` (deferred) | Reserved. Earlier drafts described snapshotting the prior synthesis as immutable `synthesis-v{generation}.md`. the runtime does not emit lineage events for `completed-continue` today. |
266: 
267: See the Lifecycle Branches section in `references/loop_protocol.md` for the canonical event contract. Legacy artifact names remain read-only migration aliases for a 4-week window. The workflow writes only canonical `deep-research-*` names and emits migration events when it consumes a legacy alias.
268: <!-- /ANCHOR:lifecycle-modes -->
269: 
270: ---
271: 
272: <!-- ANCHOR:runtime-parity -->
273: ## 8. RUNTIME PARITY
274: 
275: The workflow resolves the runtime mirror from the active CLI, but every mirror must preserve the same packet contract:
276: 
277: | Runtime | Mirror |
278: |---------|--------|
279: | OpenCode / Copilot | `.opencode/agents/deep-research.md` |
280: | Claude | `.claude/agents/deep-research.md` |
281: | Codex | `.codex/agents/deep-research.toml` |
282: | Gemini | `.gemini/agents/deep-research.md` |
283: 
284: Read `.opencode/skills/deep-research/references/capability_matrix.md` for the parity checklist and `.opencode/skills/deep-research/assets/runtime_capabilities.json` plus `.opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/runtime-capabilities.cjs` for the machine-readable lookup path.
285: <!-- /ANCHOR:runtime-parity -->
286: 
287: ---
288: 
289: <!-- ANCHOR:troubleshooting -->
290: ## 9. TROUBLESHOOTING
291: 
292: | Problem | Check |
293: |---------|-------|
294: | Packet resumes when you expected a new run | Inspect `config.lineage` and the latest lifecycle event in `deep-research-state.jsonl`. |
295: | Strategy/dashboard drift | Confirm the reducer ran and regenerated `findings-registry.json` and `deep-research-dashboard.md`. |
296: | JSONL parse failure | Run `cat research/deep-research-state.jsonl | jq .` and fall back to iteration-file reconstruction if needed. |
297: | Runtime mirror behaves differently | Compare the mirror against `references/capability_matrix.md`. |
298: | Loop will not continue after pause | Remove `research/.deep-research-pause` and restart the command so the lifecycle check can emit `resumed`. |
299: | `spec.md` write is blocked | Inspect `folder_state`, `research/.deep-research.lock`, and the conflict details defined in `references/spec_check_protocol.md`. |
300: <!-- /ANCHOR:troubleshooting -->
301: 
302: ---
303: 
304: <!-- ANCHOR:faq -->
305: ## 10. FAQ
306: 
307: **Q: Does the agent still edit `deep-research-strategy.md` directly?**
308: A: Not as the source of truth. The reducer owns the machine-managed sections so packet state stays synchronized.
309: 
310: **Q: What is the difference between `resume` and `restart`?**
311: A: `resume` continues the same `sessionId` and generation, leaving the `research/` tree in place. The workflow appends a `resumed` JSONL event. `restart` archives the existing `research/` tree under `research_archive/{timestamp}/`, mints a fresh `sessionId`, increments `generation`, and appends a `restarted` JSONL event. Both events share the full lineage-contract field set documented in the Lifecycle Branches section of `references/loop_protocol.md`.
312: 
313: **Q: What happened to `fork` and `completed-continue`?**
314: A: Both were described in earlier drafts but never shipped as runtime branches. They are deferred and the workflow no longer exposes them as options. If the long-form lineage feature is implemented later it will arrive with first-class event emission, reducer ancestry handling, and replay fixtures. until then treat each run as a standalone session or use `restart` to archive the prior one.
315: 
316: **Q: Can non-hook runtimes use the same workflow safely?**
317: A: Yes. Packet files are the authority. Hooks only improve startup ergonomics.
318: 
319: **Q: What can `/deep:start-research-loop` change in `spec.md`?**
320: A: Only the bounded mutations in `references/spec_check_protocol.md`. seed markers or pre-init context during INIT, plus one machine-owned `BEGIN GENERATED` / `END GENERATED` findings block during SYNTHESIS.
321: 
322: **Q: Where should review work go now?**
323: A: Use `deep-review` and `/deep:start-review-loop`.
324: <!-- /ANCHOR:faq -->
325: 
326: ---
327: 
328: <!-- ANCHOR:related-documents -->
329: ## 11. RELATED DOCUMENTS
330: 
331: ### Dependencies
332: 
333: `deep-research` depends on `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/` for executor + state + coverage-graph runtime, which runs in a fully isolated, no-MCP execution mode.
334: 
335: | Resource | Purpose |
336: |----------|---------|
337: | `SKILL.md` | Full protocol and rules |
338: | `references/loop_protocol.md` | Detailed lifecycle and reducer sequencing |
339: | `references/spec_check_protocol.md` | Bounded `spec.md` anchoring, `folder_state` rules, advisory lock lifecycle, and generated-fence write-back |
340: | `references/state_format.md` | State packet hub and mutability/navigation summary |
341: | `references/state_jsonl.md` | Config, iteration, event, lineage, graph, and blocked-stop records |
342: | `references/state_outputs.md` | Strategy, iteration markdown, `research.md`, dashboard, resource-map, and spec anchoring outputs |
343: | `references/state_reducer_registry.md` | Reducer ownership, findings registry, validation, reconstruction, and file protection |
344: | `references/convergence.md` | Live stop contract, legal-stop gates, and convergence navigation hub |
345: | `references/convergence_signals.md` | `newInfoRatio`, rolling average, MAD, entropy, stuck count, and reporting |
346: | `references/convergence_recovery.md` | Stuck recovery, recovery strategy selection, tiered errors, and escalation |
347: | `references/convergence_graph.md` | Graph-aware STOP gates, graph convergence events, and graceful degradation |
348: | `references/convergence_reference_only.md` | Non-executable segment, semantic, dead-end, and optimizer notes |
349: | `manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/031-graph-convergence-signals.md` | Operator test case for graph stop guards and blocked-stop behavior |
350: | `references/capability_matrix.md` | Runtime parity source of truth |
351: | `feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md` | Canonical feature inventory across loop lifecycle, state management, convergence, and research output |
352: | `deep-review` | Dedicated iterative code review skill |
353: <!-- /ANCHOR:related-documents -->

(End of file - total 353 lines)
</content>

10: 
11: # Autonomous Deep Research Loop
12: 
13: Note: `Task` is allowed for the command executor that manages the loop. The `@deep-research` agent itself is LEAF-only and does not dispatch sub-agents.
14: 
15: Iterative research protocol with fresh context per iteration, externalized state, and convergence detection for deep technical investigation.
16: 
17: Runtime path resolution:
18: - OpenCode/Copilot runtime: `.opencode/agents/*.md`
19: - Claude runtime: `.claude/agents/*.md`
20: - Codex runtime: `.codex/agents/*.toml`
21: - Gemini runtime: `.gemini/agents/*.md`
22: 
23: Operator contract precedence for this skill surface:
24: - Command entrypoint syntax in `.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md`
25: - Convergence math in `references/convergence.md` and the deep-research YAML workflow
26: - Runtime agent inventories from the checked-in runtime directories above
27: 
28: ### Convergence Threshold Semantics
29: 
30: **Default:** 0.05 on newInfoRatio (fully-new=1.0, partially-new=0.5, +0.10 simplicity bonus, capped 1.0)
31: 
32: **Semantic:** `convergenceThreshold` compares newly discovered information against accumulated research knowledge with negative-knowledge emphasis. Lower = more iterations / higher signal threshold.
33: 
34: **NOT INTERCHANGEABLE with siblings:**
35: - `deep-review` uses 0.10 default on weighted P0/P1/P2 severity ratio
36: - `deep-ai-council` (proposed) uses 0.20 default on adjudicator-verdict stability
37: 
38: Carrying threshold expectations across siblings will cause unexpected iteration counts. See this skill's changelog and decision records for the cross-sibling threshold research and parity invariants that confirm thresholds do not carry across siblings.
39: 
40: ## 1. WHEN TO USE
41: 
42: ### Activation Triggers
43: 
44: Use this skill when:
45: - Deep investigation requiring multiple rounds of discovery
46: - Topic spans 3+ technical domains or sources
47: - Initial findings need progressive refinement
48: - Overnight or unattended research sessions
49: - Research where prior findings inform subsequent queries
50: 
51: Keyword triggers:
52: 
53: - `autoresearch`
54: - `deep research`
55: - `autonomous research`
56: - `research loop`
57: - `iterative research`
58: - `multi-round research`
59: - `deep investigation`
60: - `comprehensive research`
61: 
62: ### Use Cases
63: 
64: Use deep-research for multi-round technical investigation, source triangulation, repeated exploration with fresh context, and research sessions where prior findings should shape the next focus.
65: 
66: ### When NOT to Use
67: 
68: - Simple, single-question research (use direct codebase search or `/speckit:plan`)
69: - Known-solution documentation (use `/speckit:plan`)
70: - Implementation tasks (use `/speckit:implement`)
71: - Quick codebase searches (use `@context` or direct Grep/Glob)
72: - Fewer than 3 sources needed (single-pass research suffices)
73: 
74: ---
75: 
76: ## 2. SMART ROUTING
77: 
78: > Pattern: aligned with the [sk-doc smart-router resilience template](../sk-doc/assets/skill/skill_smart_router.md).
79: 
80: ### Resource Domains
81: 
82: The router discovers markdown resources recursively from `references/` and `assets/`, then applies intent scoring from `RESOURCE_MAP`. Keep routing domain-focused rather than hardcoding exhaustive inventories.
83: 
84: - `references/quick_reference.md` for the first-touch operator cheat sheet.
85: - `references/loop_protocol.md` for lifecycle, dispatch, reducer sequencing, and command-owned state flow.
86: - `references/spec_check_protocol.md` for bounded `spec.md` anchoring and generated-fence write-back.
87: - `references/convergence*.md` for stop contracts, signals, recovery, graph gates, and reference-only convergence ideas.
88: - `references/state*.md` for packet layout, JSONL records, markdown outputs, reducer ownership, and reconstruction.
89: - `references/capability_matrix.md` for runtime parity.
90: - `assets/*.md` for markdown templates and prompt assets that are safe to route through guarded markdown loading.
91: 
92: ### Resource Loading Levels
93: 
94: | Level | When to Load | Resources |
95: |-------|--------------|-----------|
96: | ALWAYS | Every skill invocation | Quick reference baseline |
97: | CONDITIONAL | If intent signals match | Loop, convergence, state, spec anchoring, runtime parity references |
98: | ON_DEMAND | Only on explicit request | Full reference set and markdown assets |
99: 
100: ### Phase Signals
101: 
102: | Phase | Signal | Primary Resources |
103: |-------|--------|-------------------|
104: | Init | No JSONL exists or setup context | `loop_protocol.md`, `state_format.md`, `state_jsonl.md` |
105: | Iteration | Dispatch context includes iteration number | `loop_protocol.md`, `state_outputs.md`, `convergence_signals.md` |
106: | Stuck | Dispatch context includes recovery language | `convergence_recovery.md`, `state_reducer_registry.md` |
107: | Synthesis | STOP candidate or final report | `convergence.md`, `state_outputs.md`, `spec_check_protocol.md` |
108: 
109: ### Smart Router Pseudocode
110: 
111: The authoritative routing logic for scoped loading, weighted intent scoring, ambiguity handling, and graceful fallback.
112: 
113: - Pattern 1: Runtime Discovery - `discover_markdown_resources()` recursively scans `references/` and `assets/`.
114: - Pattern 2: Existence-Check Before Load - `load_if_available()` guards paths, checks `inventory`, and suppresses repeats with `seen`.
115: - Pattern 3: Extensible Routing Key - intent labels route to loop resource families without static file inventories.
116: - Pattern 4: Multi-Tier Graceful Fallback - `UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST` requests disambiguation and missing families return a helpful notice.
117: 
118: ```python
119: from pathlib import Path
120: 
121: SKILL_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
122: RESOURCE_BASES = (SKILL_ROOT / "references", SKILL_ROOT / "assets")
123: DEFAULT_RESOURCE = "references/quick_reference.md"
124: 
125: INTENT_SIGNALS = {
126:     "LOOP_SETUP": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["autoresearch", "deep research", "research loop", "autonomous research", "setup", "init"]},
127:     "ITERATION": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["iteration", "next round", "continue research", "research cycle", "delta", "focus"]},
128:     "CONVERGENCE": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["convergence", "stop condition", "diminishing returns", "legal stop", "newInfoRatio"]},
129:     "RECOVERY": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["stuck", "recovery", "timeout", "reconstruct", "blocked stop", "blocked_stop"]},
130:     "STATE": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["state file", "jsonl", "strategy", "dashboard", "registry", "lineage"]},
131:     "SPEC_ANCHORING": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["spec.md", "generated fence", "folder_state", "lock", "spec anchoring"]},
132:     "RUNTIME_PARITY": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["runtime", "capability", "parity", "codex", "claude", "gemini"]},
133:     "RESOURCE_MAP": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["resource map", "resource-map", "inventory", "coverage gate"]},
134: }
135: 
136: RESOURCE_MAP = {
137:     "LOOP_SETUP": ["references/loop_protocol.md", "references/state_format.md", "references/state_jsonl.md", "references/spec_check_protocol.md"],
138:     "ITERATION": ["references/loop_protocol.md", "references/state_outputs.md", "references/convergence_signals.md"],
139:     "CONVERGENCE": ["references/convergence.md", "references/convergence_signals.md", "references/convergence_graph.md"],
140:     "RECOVERY": ["references/convergence_recovery.md", "references/state_reducer_registry.md"],
141:     "STATE": ["references/state_format.md", "references/state_jsonl.md", "references/state_outputs.md", "references/state_reducer_registry.md"],
142:     "SPEC_ANCHORING": ["references/spec_check_protocol.md", "references/state_outputs.md"],
143:     "RUNTIME_PARITY": ["references/capability_matrix.md"],
144:     "RESOURCE_MAP": ["references/loop_protocol.md", "references/state_outputs.md"],
145: }
146: 
147: LOADING_LEVELS = {
148:     "ALWAYS": [DEFAULT_RESOURCE],
149:     "ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS": ["full protocol", "all references", "complete reference", "resume deep research", "state log", "research/iterations", "deltas", "overnight research", "active lineage", "reference-only", "optimizer"],
150:     "ON_DEMAND": [
151:         "references/loop_protocol.md",
152:         "references/spec_check_protocol.md",
153:         "references/convergence.md",
154:         "references/convergence_signals.md",
155:         "references/convergence_recovery.md",
156:         "references/convergence_graph.md",
157:         "references/convergence_reference_only.md",
158:         "references/state_format.md",
159:         "references/state_jsonl.md",
160:         "references/state_outputs.md",
161:         "references/state_reducer_registry.md",
162:         "references/capability_matrix.md",
163:     ],
164: }
165: 
166: UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST = [
167:     "Confirm setup vs iteration vs convergence vs state recovery",
168:     "Confirm the target spec folder and research packet",
169:     "Provide the current phase, latest iteration, or failing state file",
170:     "Confirm whether full references or quick routing guidance are needed",
171: ]
172: 
173: def _task_text(task) -> str:
174:     return " ".join([
175:         str(getattr(task, "text", "")),
176:         str(getattr(task, "query", "")),
177:         " ".join(getattr(task, "keywords", []) or []),
178:     ]).lower()
179: 
180: def _guard_in_skill(relative_path: str) -> str:
181:     resolved = (SKILL_ROOT / relative_path).resolve()
182:     resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT)
183:     if resolved.suffix.lower() != ".md":
184:         raise ValueError(f"Only markdown resources are routable: {relative_path}")
185:     return resolved.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix()
186: 
187: def discover_markdown_resources() -> set[str]:
188:     docs = []
189:     for base in RESOURCE_BASES:
190:         if base.exists():
191:             docs.extend(path for path in base.rglob("*.md") if path.is_file())
192:     return {doc.relative_to(SKILL_ROOT).as_posix() for doc in docs}
193: 
194: def score_intents(task) -> dict[str, float]:
195:     text = _task_text(task)
196:     scores = {intent: 0.0 for intent in INTENT_SIGNALS}
197:     for intent, cfg in INTENT_SIGNALS.items():
198:         for keyword in cfg["keywords"]:
199:             if keyword in text:
200:                 scores[intent] += cfg["weight"]
201:     return scores
202: 
203: def select_intents(scores: dict[str, float], ambiguity_delta: float = 1.0, max_intents: int = 2) -> list[str]:
204:     ranked = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda item: item[1], reverse=True)
205:     if not ranked or ranked[0][1] <= 0:
206:         return ["LOOP_SETUP"]
207:     selected = [ranked[0][0]]
208:     if len(ranked) > 1 and ranked[1][1] > 0 and (ranked[0][1] - ranked[1][1]) <= ambiguity_delta:
209:         selected.append(ranked[1][0])
210:     return selected[:max_intents]
211: 
212: def route_deep_research_resources(task):
213:     inventory = discover_markdown_resources()
214:     scores = score_intents(task)
215:     intents = select_intents(scores)
216:     loaded = []
217:     seen = set()
218: 
219:     def load_if_available(relative_path: str) -> None:
220:         guarded = _guard_in_skill(relative_path)
221:         if guarded in inventory and guarded not in seen:
222:             load(guarded)
223:             loaded.append(guarded)
224:             seen.add(guarded)
225: 
226:     for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ALWAYS"]:
227:         load_if_available(relative_path)
228: 
229:     if max(scores.values() or [0]) < 0.5:
230:         return {
231:             "intents": intents,
232:             "intent_scores": scores,
233:             "load_level": "UNKNOWN_FALLBACK",
234:             "needs_disambiguation": True,
235:             "disambiguation_checklist": UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST,
236:             "resources": loaded,
237:         }
238: 
239:     matched_intents = []
240:     for intent in intents:
241:         before_count = len(loaded)
242:         for relative_path in RESOURCE_MAP.get(intent, []):
243:             load_if_available(relative_path)
244:         if len(loaded) > before_count:
245:             matched_intents.append(intent)
246: 
247:     text = _task_text(task)
248:     if any(keyword in text for keyword in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS"]):
249:         for relative_path in LOADING_LEVELS["ON_DEMAND"]:
250:             load_if_available(relative_path)
251: 
252:     result = {"intents": intents, "intent_scores": scores, "resources": loaded}
253:     if not matched_intents:
254:         result["notice"] = f"No knowledge base found for intent(s): {', '.join(intents)}"
255:     return result
256: ```
257: 
258: ---
259: 
260: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS
261: 
262: ### Invocation Contract
263: 
264: This skill is invoked exclusively through `/deep:start-research-loop:auto` or `/deep:start-research-loop:confirm`. The command YAML owns state, dispatch, convergence, and synthesis.
265: 
266: Never simulate the loop with ad hoc shell dispatch, nested CLI loops, direct `@deep-research` Task dispatch, `/tmp` prompt files, or state outside the resolved local research packet.
267: 
268: ### Executor Selection Contract
269: 
270: The YAML workflow owns executor selection. Native `@deep-research` is the default path; CLI executors are routed through the workflow, not through ad hoc shell loops.
271: 
272: Cross-CLI delegation is possible inside each executor sandbox but discouraged. Do not invoke the same CLI from within itself, and do not assume auth from a parent executor propagates to child CLIs.
273: 
274: Executor invariants:
275: 
276: 1. Produce a non-empty iteration markdown file at `{state_paths.iteration_pattern}`.
277: 2. Append a JSONL delta record to `{state_paths.state_log}` with required fields: `type`, `iteration`, `newInfoRatio`, `status`, and `focus`.
278: 3. Respect the LEAF-agent constraint: no sub-dispatch, no nested loops, and max 12 tool calls per iteration.
279: 
280: Failure modes include `iteration_file_missing`, `iteration_file_empty`, `jsonl_not_appended`, `jsonl_missing_fields`, and `jsonl_parse_error`. Three consecutive failures route to stuck recovery.
281: 
282: ### Lifecycle Contract
283: 
284: Runtime-supported lifecycle modes:
285: 
286: | Mode | Meaning |
287: |------|---------|
288: | `new` | First run against the spec folder |
289: | `resume` | Continue the active lineage and append a typed `resumed` JSONL event |
290: | `restart` | Archive the existing research tree, mint a fresh `sessionId`, increment `generation`, and append a typed `restarted` event |
291: 
292: Deferred modes are `fork` and `completed-continue`. They are reserved but not runtime-supported.
293: 
294: ### Code-Graph Readiness TrustState Surface
295: 
296: The live code-graph readiness contract reaches four TrustState values: `live`, `stale`, `absent`, and `unavailable`.
297: 
298: `cached`, `imported`, `rebuilt`, and `rehomed` remain declared in the shared TrustState type for compatibility, but the readiness helpers used here do not emit them today.
299: 
300: ### Resource Map Integration
301: 
302: When `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md` exists at init, deep research promotes it from ad hoc context to canonical packet state.
303: 
304: - Persist `resource_map_present: true` in `deep-research-config.json`.
305: - Read the map once during init and summarize it into `deep-research-strategy.md` `Known Context`.
306: - The snapshot MUST include per-section entry counts across `READMEs`, `Documents`, `Commands`, `Agents`, `Skills`, `Specs`, `Scripts`, `Tests`, `Config`, and `Meta`.
307: - The snapshot MUST also include a one-line theme summary for each resource-map section so later iterations inherit the map's structure without rereading the entire file.
308: - Per-iteration prompts surface `resource-map.md` as the exclusion set for previously inventoried files.
309: - Treat files already listed on the map as known inventory, not as net-new discoveries.
310: - Only flag files as gaps when they look relevant to the active investigation and are missing from the map.
311: - Final synthesis cites `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md` in `research.md` References when the map was present at init.
312: - Convergence also emits `{artifact_dir}/resource-map.md` from research delta evidence unless the operator passes `--no-resource-map`.
313: 
314: When `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md` is absent at init:
315: 
316: - Persist `resource_map_present: false`.
317: - Write `resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate` into `Known Context`.
318: - Omit the exclusion-set hint from iteration guidance.
319: - Continue the loop normally; absence is informational, not a failure.
320: 
321: ### Architecture: 3-Layer Integration
322: 
323: `/deep:start-research-loop` owns the YAML workflow, which initializes state, dispatches one LEAF iteration at a time, evaluates convergence, synthesizes `research/research.md`, and saves continuity. The `@deep-research` agent executes only one research cycle per dispatch.
324: 
325: ### State Packet Location
326: 
327: The research state packet always lives under the target spec's local `research/` folder. Root-spec targets use `{spec_folder}/research/` directly. Child-phase and sub-phase targets use **flat-first**: a first run with an empty `research/` directory writes flat at `{spec_folder}/research/`. A `pt-NN` subfolder (`{basename(spec_folder)}-pt-{NN}`) is allocated only when prior content already exists in `research/` for a non-matching target (continuation runs reuse the existing flat artifact or matching `pt-NN` packet). This avoids the unnecessary `pt-01` wrapper on first runs.
328: 
329: Example (first run on a child phase): `.../026-graph.../019-system-hardening/001-initial-research/004-desc-regen/` writes to `004-desc-regen/research/` directly.
330: 
331: Example (subsequent run with prior content for a different target): `004-desc-regen/research/004-desc-regen-pt-02/` (pt-NN allocated as a sibling to the prior content).
332: 
333: State files include `deep-research-config.json`, `deep-research-state.jsonl`, `deep-research-strategy.md`, `deep-research-findings-registry.json`, `deep-research-dashboard.md`, `.deep-research-pause`, `.deep-research.lock`, `resource-map.md`, `research.md`, and `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`.
334: 
335: v(next): `deep-research-findings-registry.json` is the canonical registry name for sibling-skill consistency.
336: 
337: ### Core Innovation: Fresh Context Per Iteration
338: 
339: Each agent dispatch gets a fresh context window. State continuity comes from files, not memory. This solves context degradation in long research sessions.
340: 
341: Adapted from: karpathy/autoresearch (loop concept), AGR (fresh context "Ralph Loop"), pi-autoresearch (JSONL state), autoresearch-opencode (context injection).
342: 
343: ### Data Flow
344: 
345: Init creates config, strategy, and state logs. Each loop reads state, checks convergence, dispatches `@deep-research`, writes iteration markdown and JSONL deltas, refreshes reducer-owned state, and either continues or synthesizes and saves continuity.
346: 
347: Late-INIT can also anchor the research run to `spec.md`: the workflow acquires the advisory lock, classifies `folder_state`, seeds or appends bounded context before LOOP, and then syncs one generated findings block back into `spec.md` during SYNTHESIS while keeping `research/research.md` canonical.
348: 
349: That contract is exact:
350: - `folder_state` is always one of `no-spec`, `spec-present`, `spec-just-created-by-this-run`, or `conflict-detected`
351: - the advisory lock lives at `research/.deep-research.lock` from late INIT through save, skip-save, or cancel cleanup
352: - post-synthesis write-back replaces exactly one `<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: deep-research/spec-findings -->` ... `<!-- END GENERATED: deep-research/spec-findings -->` fence under the chosen host anchor
353: - the full bounded mutation rules live in [spec_check_protocol.md](references/spec_check_protocol.md)
354: 
355: ### Key Concepts
356: 
357: Research continuity is externalized to files, each iteration starts fresh, convergence uses newInfoRatio/stuck/question signals, strategy and registry are reducer-owned, JSONL remains append-only, and final synthesis consolidates `research/research.md`.
358: 
359: ---
360: 
361: ## 4. RULES
362: 
363: ### ALWAYS
364: 
365: 1. **Read state first** -- Agent must read JSONL and strategy.md before any research action
366: 2. **One focus per iteration** -- Pick ONE research sub-topic from strategy.md "Next Focus"
367: 3. **Externalize findings** -- Write to iteration-NNN.md, not held in agent context
368: 4. **Update strategy** -- Append to "What Worked"/"What Failed", update "Next Focus"
369: 5. **Report newInfoRatio** -- Every iteration JSONL record must include newInfoRatio
370: 6. **Respect exhausted approaches** -- Never retry approaches in the "Exhausted" list
371: 7. **Cite sources** -- Every finding must cite `[SOURCE: url]` or `[SOURCE: file:line]`
372: 8. **Use generate-context.js for memory saves** -- Never manually create memory files
373: 9. **Treat research/research.md as workflow-owned** -- Iteration findings feed synthesis; the workflow owns the canonical `research/research.md`
374: 10. **Document ruled-out directions per iteration** -- Every iteration must include what was tried and failed
375: 11. **Report newInfoRatio + 1-sentence novelty justification** -- Every JSONL iteration record must include both
376: 12. **Quality guards must pass before convergence** -- Source diversity, focus alignment, and no single-weak-source checks must pass before STOP can trigger
377: 13. **Respect reducer ownership** -- The workflow reducer, not the agent, is the source of truth for strategy machine-owned sections, dashboard metrics, and findings registry updates
378: 14. **Use canonical packet names only** -- Write `deep-research-*` artifacts and `research/.deep-research-pause`; legacy names are read-only migration aliases
379: 15. **Invoke through the command workflow** -- Use `/deep:start-research-loop:auto` or `/deep:start-research-loop:confirm`, and let the YAML workflow own dispatch
380: 
381: ### NEVER
382: 
383: 1. **Dispatch sub-agents** -- @deep-research is LEAF-only (NDP compliance)
384: 2. **Hold findings in context** -- Write everything to files
385: 3. **Exceed TCB** -- Target 8-11 tool calls per iteration (max 12)
386: 4. **Ask the user** -- Autonomous execution; make best-judgment decisions
387: 5. **Skip convergence checks** -- Every iteration must be evaluated
388: 6. **Modify config after init** -- Config is read-only after initialization
389: 7. **Overwrite prior findings** -- Append to research/research.md, never replace
390: 8. **Implement fixes during research** -- Report findings only; implementation is a separate follow-up step.
391: 9. **Simulate loop dispatch** -- Do not write custom shell loops, nested CLI loops, `/tmp` prompt dispatchers, or direct Task loops for `@deep-research`
392: 
393: ### Iteration Status Enum
394: 
395: `complete | timeout | error | stuck | insight | thought`
396: 
397: - `insight`: Low newInfoRatio but important conceptual breakthrough
398: - `thought`: Analytical-only iteration, no evidence gathering
399: 
400: ### EXPERIMENTAL / REFERENCE-ONLY FEATURES
401: 
402: These concepts remain documented for future design work, but they are not part of the live executable contract for `/deep:start-research-loop`:
403: 1. **Wave orchestration** -- parallel question fan-out, pruning, and breakthrough logic
404: 2. **Checkpoint commits** -- per-iteration git commits
405: 3. **Wave orchestration on the same lineage** -- parallel fan-out remains reference-only
406: 4. **Alternate CLI dispatch** -- process-isolated `claude -p` or similar dispatch modes
407: 
408: ### ESCALATE IF
409: 
410: 1. **3+ consecutive timeouts** -- Infrastructure issue, not research problem
411: 2. **State file corruption unrecoverable** -- Cannot reconstruct from JSONL or iteration files
412: 3. **All approaches exhausted with questions remaining** -- Research may need human guidance
413: 4. **Security concern in findings** -- Proprietary code or credentials discovered
414: 5. **All recovery tiers exhausted** -- No automatic recovery path remaining
415: 
416: ---
417: 
418: ## 5. REFERENCES
419: 
420: Core documentation: `references/quick_reference.md`, `references/loop_protocol.md`, `references/spec_check_protocol.md`, `references/convergence.md`, and `references/state_format.md`.
421: 
422: Focused convergence references: `references/convergence_signals.md`, `references/convergence_recovery.md`, `references/convergence_graph.md`, and `references/convergence_reference_only.md`.
423: 
424: Focused state references: `references/state_jsonl.md`, `references/state_outputs.md`, and `references/state_reducer_registry.md`.
425: 
426: Templates: `assets/deep_research_config.json`, `assets/deep_research_strategy.md`, `assets/deep_research_dashboard.md`, `assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl`, and `assets/runtime_capabilities.json`.
427: 
428: Cross-skill alignment: `deep-research` owns iterative investigation. Its shared resource family mirrors `deep-review` and `deep-ai-council`, but its vocabulary remains novelty, sources, negative knowledge, question coverage, and research synthesis rather than severity findings or council agreement.
429: 
430: ---
431: 
432: ## 6. SUCCESS CRITERIA
433: 
434: ### Loop Completion
435: - Research loop ran to convergence or max iterations
436: - All state files present and consistent (config, JSONL, strategy)
437: - research/resource-map.md produced from converged deltas unless `config.resource_map.emit == false` (operator flag: `--no-resource-map`)
438: - research/research.md produced with findings from all iterations
439: - Canonical continuity surfaces updated via generate-context.js
440: 
441: ### Quality Gates
442: 
443: Blocking gates: valid config/strategy/state before loop, iteration markdown plus JSONL plus reducer refresh per iteration, final `research/research.md` and convergence report after loop, and quality guards for source diversity/focus/no weak single source. Continuity save is expected but non-blocking.
444: 
445: ### Convergence Report
446: 
447: Every completed loop produces a convergence report:
448: - Stop reason (converged, max_iterations, all_questions_answered, stuck_unrecoverable)
449: - Total iterations completed
450: - Questions answered ratio
451: - Average newInfoRatio trend
452: 
453: ---
454: 
455: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS
456: 
457: ### Framework Integration
458: 
459: This skill operates within the behavioral framework defined in the active runtime's root doc (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CODEX.md, or GEMINI.md).
460: 
461: Key integrations:
462: - **Gate 2**: Skill routing via `skill_advisor.py` (keywords: autoresearch, deep research)
463: - **Gate 3**: File modifications require spec folder question per the root doc Gate 3
464: - **Continuity**: `/speckit:resume` is the operator-facing recovery surface; canonical packet continuity is written via `generate-context.js`
465: - **Orchestrator**: @orchestrate dispatches @deep-research as LEAF agent
466: 
467: ### Continuity Integration
468: 
469: Before research, recover context with `/speckit:resume` using `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`. During each iteration, write `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`, append JSONL, and let the reducer refresh strategy/registry/dashboard. After research, save continuity through `generate-context.js`.
470: 
471: ### Command Integration
472: 
473: | Command | Relationship |
474: |---------|-------------|
475: | `/deep:start-research-loop` | Primary invocation point |
476: | `/speckit:resume` | Canonical recovery surface before resuming or extending an active packet |
477: | `/speckit:plan` | Next step after deep research completes |
478: | `/memory:save` | Manual memory save (deep research auto-saves) |
479: 
480: ---
481: 
482: ## 8. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES
483: 
484: The router discovers reference and markdown asset docs dynamically. Start with `references/quick_reference.md`, then route to loop protocol, spec anchoring, convergence, state, runtime parity, or recovery references based on intent.
485: 
486: Scripts: `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`, `scripts/runtime-capabilities.cjs`.
487: 
488: Related skills: `deep-review` for iterative audit loops, `deep-loop-runtime` for shared executor/state/coverage-graph runtime, and `system-spec-kit` for command-owned state, packet anchoring, and continuity saves.

(End of file - total 488 lines)
</content>

Note: `Task` is allowed for the command executor that manages the loop. The `@deep-research` agent itself is LEAF-only and does not dispatch sub-agents.

Iterative research protocol with fresh context per iteration, externalized state, and convergence detection for deep technical investigation.

36: ---
37: 
38: <!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
39: ## 1. OVERVIEW
40: 
41: ### Purpose
42: 
43: `deep-review` solves a problem single-pass review tools cannot: thorough, dimension-balanced code audits where context degradation across long sessions would otherwise bias the verdict. Each iteration dispatches a clean `@deep-review` agent that reads strategy and state from disk, picks one focus dimension, emits findings to JSONL, and exits. The orchestrating command checks a three-signal convergence vote between iterations and stops the loop only when severity-weighted findings stabilize, all required dimensions are covered, and three binary quality gates pass.
44: 
45: The agent is read-only against the review target. All state lives in a dedicated `{spec_folder}/review/` packet: an immutable config, an append-only JSONL log, a mutable strategy file, reducer-owned dashboards and registries, and per-iteration markdown notes. Recovery, pause-resume, and forensic replay all read from disk, not from memory.
46: 
47: When the review hands control back to general packet work, `/speckit:resume` rebuilds context in the canonical order (`handover.md`, `_memory.continuity`, then the rest of the packet docs). Memory artifacts generated by the loop stay secondary.
48: 
49: ### Usage
50: 
51: Read this README to learn what the skill does, how to invoke it, how to read its outputs, and how to recover when something goes wrong. The runtime contract for the loop itself lives in `SKILL.md` and the four `references/` deep-dives. This README links into them at every point where a reader needs the next level of detail.
52: 
53: ### Key Statistics
54: 
55: | Metric | Value |
56: |---|---|
57: | Version | 1.10.1.0 |
58: | Operating modes | 2 (`:auto`, `:confirm`) |
59: | Lifecycle modes | 3 active (`new`, `resume`, `restart`) + 2 reserved (`fork`, `completed-continue`) |
60: | Review dimensions | 4 (Correctness, Security, Traceability, Maintainability) |
61: | Severity levels | 3 (P0 / P1 / P2) with weights 10.0 / 5.0 / 1.0 |
62: | Quality gates | 3 binary (evidence, scope, coverage) + claim-adjudication gate |
63: | Verdicts | PASS, CONDITIONAL, FAIL |
64: | Default max iterations | 7 |
65: | Default convergence threshold | 0.10 (severity-weighted ratio) |
66: | Stuck recovery threshold | 3 consecutive no-progress iterations |
67: | State files per packet | 7 primary + per-iteration markdown |
68: | Tool budget per iteration | 9 to 12 (max 13) |
69: | Output | `{spec_folder}/review/review-report.md` (9 sections) |
70: 
71: ### How This Compares
72: 
73: | Capability | `deep-review` | `sk-code-review` |
74: |---|---|---|
75: | Review pattern | Iterative multi-pass until convergence | Single pass |
76: | Context model | Fresh per iteration, state on disk | One context for the whole review |
77: | Severity model | P0 / P1 / P2 weighted convergence | Findings list without weighted stop |
78: | Dimension tracking | 4-dimension coverage gate | No coverage tracking |
79: | Adversarial check | Hunter / Skeptic / Referee on every P0 | None |
80: | Verdict | PASS, CONDITIONAL, FAIL (binary release gate) | Findings only |
81: | Best for | Release gates, audits where misses are expensive | Quick feedback during active development |
82: 
83: ### Key Features
84: 
85: - **Three-signal convergence vote** combining severity-weighted new findings, rolling stop average, and dimension coverage. STOP is legal only when all three vote together and the quality-gate bundle passes.
86: - **P0 override** forces at least one more iteration on any new P0, regardless of other signals.
87: - **Adversarial self-check** on every P0 before it enters the active findings registry.
88: - **Semantic coverage graph** persists per-iteration `graphEvents` so structural blind spots block premature STOP.
89: - **Resource-map emission** writes `{spec_folder}/review/resource-map.md` from converged delta evidence. Operators can disable per run with `--no-resource-map`.
90: - **Fail-closed reducer** refuses to write derived state when JSONL corruption is detected.
91: - **Auto-resume** detects existing state on re-invocation and continues from the last completed iteration.
92: - **Pause sentinel** halts between iterations when the operator creates `.deep-review-pause`.
93: 
94: <!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
95: 
96: ---
97: 
98: <!-- ANCHOR:quick-start -->
99: ## 2. QUICK START
100: 
101: **Step 1: Invoke the loop.**
102: 
103: Pick `:auto` for unattended runs or `:confirm` for approval gates between iterations:
104: 
105: ```bash
106: # Autonomous audit of a skill package
107: /deep:start-review-loop:auto "skill deep-review"
108: 
109: # Interactive audit with approval at each step
110: /deep:start-review-loop:confirm "<spec-folder>"
111: 
112: # Autonomous audit of every spec folder in a track
113: /deep:start-review-loop:auto "track skilled-agent-orchestration"
114: ```
115: 
116: Expected result: the YAML workflow creates `{spec_folder}/review/` and dispatches the first iteration.
117: 
118: **Step 2: Monitor or pause.**
119: 
120: Watch the dashboard between iterations:
121: 
122: ```bash
123: cat {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-dashboard.md
124: ```
125: 
126: Pause cleanly by creating the sentinel:
127: 
128: ```bash
129: touch {spec_folder}/review/.deep-review-pause
130: ```
131: 
132: Delete the sentinel to resume.
133: 
134: **Step 3: Read the verdict.**
135: 
136: After convergence (or max iterations), the loop writes the synthesized report:
137: 
138: ```bash
139: grep -E "^Release Readiness Verdict|^## " {spec_folder}/review/review-report.md
140: ```
141: 
142: Expected result: nine section headers plus a verdict line of `PASS`, `CONDITIONAL`, or `FAIL`.
143: 
144: <!-- /ANCHOR:quick-start -->
145: 
146: ---
147: 
148: <!-- ANCHOR:features -->
149: ## 3. FEATURES
150: 
151: ### 3.1 FEATURE HIGHLIGHTS
152: 
153: The loop runs in four phases (initialization, iteration, synthesis, save) with state externalized to seven files in `{spec_folder}/review/`. Each iteration is a single LEAF dispatch that focuses on one review dimension, emits findings with file:line evidence, appends a JSONL delta, and updates the strategy file. The orchestrating command evaluates convergence between iterations using three signals (severity-weighted new findings, rolling stop average, dimension coverage) that vote together. STOP is legal only when the vote passes AND three binary quality gates (evidence, scope, coverage) plus the claim-adjudication gate all clear.
154: 
155: Every P0 finding runs through an adversarial Hunter / Skeptic / Referee self-check before it enters the active registry. New P0s force at least one more iteration regardless of other signals. The reducer fails closed on JSONL corruption, refusing to publish derived state until the contract is repaired. A semantic coverage graph keyed by `loop_type='review'` lets convergence cross-check structural blind spots against the dimension vote.
156: 
157: Synthesis dedups findings across iterations using content-hash plus file:line, reconciles claim-adjudicated severities, and writes the nine-section `review-report.md`. A convergence-time `resource-map.md` is emitted by default from converged delta evidence. Operators can disable per run with `--no-resource-map`. Auto-resume reads existing state on re-invocation and continues without re-running prior iterations.
158:

208: ## 4. STRUCTURE
209: 
210: ### Skill Package
211: 
212: ```text
213: deep-review/
214: +-- SKILL.md                          # Runtime instructions and smart router
215: +-- README.md                         # This file
216: +-- references/                       # Loaded protocol guidance
217: |   +-- convergence.md                # Stop algorithms, signal math, security-sensitive overrides
218: |   +-- convergence_signals.md        # Focused STOP signals, P0 override, legal-stop summary
219: |   +-- loop_protocol.md              # 4-phase lifecycle, lineage modes, dispatch contract
220: |   +-- quick_reference.md            # One-page operator cheat sheet
221: |   +-- state_format.md               # Canonical schemas for every state file
222: |   +-- state_outputs.md              # Packet outputs, dashboard, iteration markdown, report
223: |   `-- state_reducer_registry.md     # Reducer ownership, registry, reconstruction
224: +-- assets/                           # Templates and config payloads
225: |   +-- deep_review_config.json       # Default config shape
226: |   +-- deep_review_dashboard.md      # Dashboard template
227: |   +-- deep_review_strategy.md       # Strategy template
228: |   +-- prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl # Iteration prompt (rendered raw by prompt-pack.ts)
229: |   +-- review_mode_contract.yaml     # Dimensions, severities, verdicts, gates, protocols
230: |   `-- runtime_capabilities.json     # Per-runtime capability matrix
231: +-- feature_catalog/                  # 21 markdown files across 4 categories
232: +-- manual_testing_playbook/          # 45 deterministic test scenarios across 8 categories
233: +-- changelog/                        # Per-release notes (no frontmatter, no version header)
234: `-- scripts/                          # Runtime helpers (bug-scan only, no behavioral edits)
235:     +-- README.md
236:     +-- reduce-state.cjs              # State reducer (registry, dashboard, strategy)
237:     +-- runtime-capabilities.cjs      # Runtime capability detection
238:     `-- tests/fixtures/               # Reducer fixtures (blocked-stop session)
239: ```
240: 
241: ### Review Runtime State
242: 
243: Created under the resolved `{artifact_dir}` during initialization. First runs with an empty target-local `review/` folder write flat at `{spec_folder}/review/`, including child-phase and sub-phase targets. A `{spec_folder}/review/{ownerSlug}-pt-NN/` packet is allocated only when prior local content already exists for a different target. Continuations reuse the existing flat artifact or matching packet. The review target is never written to.
244: 
245: ```text
246: {spec_folder}/review/
247: +-- deep-review-config.json             # Immutable after init: loop parameters + lineage metadata
248: +-- deep-review-state.jsonl             # Append-only structured log of every iteration
249: +-- deep-review-findings-registry.json  # Reducer-owned canonical findings state
250: +-- deep-review-strategy.md             # Mutable: dimensions, findings, next focus
251: +-- deep-review-dashboard.md            # Auto-generated after each iteration
252: +-- .deep-review-pause                  # Sentinel: create to halt, delete to resume
253: +-- review-report.md                    # 9-section findings-first synthesis with verdict
254: +-- resource-map.md                     # Emitted at convergence unless --no-resource-map
255: +-- iterations/
256: |   `-- iteration-NNN.md                # Write-once per-iteration detailed findings
257: `-- {ownerSlug}-pt-NN/                  # Conditional: prior non-matching content exists
258: ```
259: 
260: ### Agent Definitions
261: 
262: | Runtime | Path |
263: |---|---|
264: | OpenCode / Copilot | `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` |
265: | Claude | `.claude/agents/deep-review.md` |
266: | Codex | `.codex/agents/deep-review.toml` |
267: | Gemini | `.gemini/agents/deep-review.md` |

273: <!-- ANCHOR:configuration -->
274: ## 5. CONFIGURATION
275: 
276: ### Command Parameters
277: 
278: | Parameter | Default | Purpose |
279: |---|---|---|
280: | `--max-iterations` | 7 | Hard cap on loop iterations |
281: | `--convergence` | 0.10 | STOP threshold on the severity-weighted `newFindingsRatio` |
282: | `--spec-folder` | auto-detected | Target spec folder for state and output |
283: | `--dimensions` | all 4 | Comma-separated subset to restrict the active dimensions |
284: | `--no-resource-map` | off | Disable convergence-time `resource-map.md` emission for one run |
285: | `--executor` | native | Optional CLI executor (e.g. `cli-codex`), see `references/loop_protocol.md` |
286: 
287: ### Default Configuration
288: 
289: | Setting | Value |
290: |---|---|
291: | Max iterations | 7 |
292: | Convergence threshold | 0.10 |
293: | Rolling stop threshold | 0.08 |
294: | No-progress threshold | 0.05 |
295: | Stuck threshold | 2 consecutive no-progress iterations |
296: | Minimum stabilization passes | 1 (2 under security-sensitive override) |
297: | Composite stop score | 0.60 |
298: | Tool budget per iteration | 9 to 12 (max 13) |
299: 
300: Threshold semantics and sibling-parity notes (deep-review 0.10 vs deep-research 0.05 vs deep-ai-council 0.20) live in [`references/convergence.md`](./references/convergence.md) §1 under "Threshold Semantics and Sibling Parity".
301: 
302: ### Review Dimensions
303: 
304: | Priority | ID | Checks |
305: |---|---|---|
306: | 1 | `correctness` | Logic, off-by-one, return types, broken invariants, edge cases |
307: | 2 | `security` | Injection, auth bypass, secrets exposure, unsafe deserialization, trust boundaries |
308: | 3 | `traceability` | Spec/code alignment, checklist evidence, cross-reference integrity |
309: | 4 | `maintainability` | Codebase patterns, documentation quality, clarity, follow-on change cost |
310: 
311: ### Target Types
312: 
313: | Type | Description |
314: |---|---|
315: | `spec-folder` | One spec folder + referenced implementation + tests + release artifacts |
316: | `skill` | Skill package: `SKILL.md`, references, assets, scripts, runtime integrations |
317: | `agent` | Named agent across runtime-specific definitions, with capability-parity surfaces |
318: | `track` | Spec track spanning multiple child folders + shared coordination artifacts |
319: | `files` | Explicit file set + immediate cross-references required to evaluate shipped behavior |
320: 
321: ### Tuning Guide
322: 
323: | Goal | Adjustment |
324: |---|---|
325: | Deeper audit | Lower `--convergence` to 0.05, raise `--max-iterations` to 10 |
326: | Faster gate check | Raise `--convergence` to 0.15, lower `--max-iterations` to 4 |
327: | Security-focused audit | Pass `--dimensions correctness,security` to restrict scope |
328: | Skip the resource map | Pass `--no-resource-map` for the run |
329: 
330: ### Security-Sensitive Override
331: 
332: Runs that target security, path handling, env precedence, schema boundaries, persistence, or shared policy automatically raise the bar: `minStabilizationPasses=2`, closed-finding replay is required, and the fix-completeness gate is enforced. STOP is not legal until the review report contains a closed-gate replay table that marks each prior active or remediated P0/P1 as `PASS`, `FAIL`, or `carried forward`, with file:line or command evidence. Full contract in [`references/convergence.md`](./references/convergence.md).
333: 
334: ### Pause and Resume
335: 
336: Create a file at `{spec_folder}/review/.deep-review-pause` during a running loop to halt between iterations. Delete the file to resume. Auto-resume detects existing JSONL state on re-invocation and continues from the last completed iteration without re-running prior work.
337:

379: <!-- ANCHOR:troubleshooting -->
380: ## 7. TROUBLESHOOTING
381: 
382: | What you see | Likely cause | Fix |
383: |---|---|---|
384: | Loop stuck on one dimension | Strategy `next-focus` not advancing after the dimension completed | Read `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-strategy.md`, if the dimension shows as complete, manually update `completed-dimensions` and re-run. If not, dispatch one more iteration. |
385: | P0 findings keep blocking convergence | Real P0s surviving adversarial check, or misclassified P1s elevated to P0 | Open the relevant `iterations/iteration-NNN.md`, read the Hunter / Skeptic / Referee output. If misclassified, downgrade severity in the JSONL entry and re-run convergence evaluation. |
386: | `review-report.md` missing sections | Synthesis step interrupted before all 9 sections wrote | Re-run synthesis manually, JSONL state is intact, synthesis reads from it without re-running iterations. |
387: | Loop runs to max iterations without converging | Convergence threshold too tight, or target has genuine structural issues | Raise `--convergence` to 0.15 and re-run with auto-resume. If findings keep appearing, the target needs remediation before review can converge. |
388: | Agent attempts a sub-agent dispatch | LEAF constraint violated in the agent definition | Verify `@deep-review` agent definition forbids the `Task` tool. Reload the runtime mirror file for the active CLI. |
389: | Verdict appears wrong | P0 severity misclassified or active-findings count incorrect | Confirm adversarial self-check ran on the P0 in the relevant iteration file. Check the JSONL `synthesis_complete` event for `activeP0` accuracy. |
390: | Reducer refuses to write derived state | JSONL corruption detected by `reduce-state.cjs` | Read the corruption-warning output. Repair the JSONL line, or re-run with `--lenient` to skip the corrupt entry, `corruptionWarnings` stays visible in reducer state. |
391: | `resource-map.md` not produced at convergence | `--no-resource-map` was passed, or `config.resource_map.emit == false` | Re-run without `--no-resource-map`, or flip the config flag and re-run synthesis. |
392: 
393: <!-- /ANCHOR:troubleshooting -->

397: <!-- ANCHOR:faq -->
398: ## 8. FAQ
399: 
400: **Q: How is `deep-review` different from `sk-code-review`?**
401: 
402: A: `sk-code-review` is a single-pass review. `deep-review` runs multiple iterations until convergence, tracks dimension coverage, runs adversarial self-checks on P0 findings, and produces a structured nine-section `review-report.md` with a three-way verdict. Use `sk-code-review` for quick feedback during active development. Use `deep-review` for release gates and audits where missed findings are expensive.
403: 
404: **Q: Can I review individual files?**
405: 
406: A: Yes. Set the target type to `files` and pass the explicit file list at invocation. The loop scopes all findings and cross-reference checks to the declared file set plus their immediate cross-references.
407: 
408: **Q: What triggers the P0 override?**
409: 
410: A: Any new P0 finding within an iteration sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50` regardless of other signals. This forces at least one more iteration. The override is a hard convergence guard and cannot be suppressed.
411: 
412: **Q: Does the review modify the target code?**
413: 
414: A: No. The `@deep-review` agent is strictly read-only against the review target. It writes only to the dedicated `{spec_folder}/review/` packet: strategy, JSONL state, dashboard, iteration files, the final report, and (by default) `resource-map.md`.
415: 
416: **Q: What happens after a FAIL verdict?**
417: 
418: A: Release is blocked. Run `/speckit:plan` to create a remediation plan from the findings in `{spec_folder}/review/review-report.md`, implement the fixes, then re-run `deep-review` to confirm resolution. The re-run uses auto-resume and continues from prior state.
419: 
420: **Q: What does CONDITIONAL mean?**
421: 
422: A: Active P0 is zero but active P1 findings remain. The code can proceed to planning and remediation but should not ship until P1s are resolved. Run `/speckit:plan` with the review report as input.
423: 
424: **Q: Can I pause a running autonomous review?**
425: 
426: A: Yes. Create a file at `{spec_folder}/review/.deep-review-pause` during the loop. The orchestrator checks for this sentinel between iterations and halts cleanly. Delete the file to continue from the last completed iteration.
427: 
428: **Q: How do I extend the review after convergence?**
429: 
430: A: Raise `--max-iterations` above the number of completed iterations and re-invoke. Auto-resume detects the existing JSONL state and continues without repeating prior iterations.
431: 
432: **Q: Where does the `graphEvents` data go?**
433: 
434: A: Each iteration appends `graphEvents` to its JSONL record. The coverage-graph reducer at [`.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts`](../deep-loop-runtime/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts) ingests them under `loop_type='review'`, keyed by `(spec_folder, session_id, iteration)`. Convergence can then cross-check the graph for unresolved coverage gaps before allowing STOP.
435: 
436: <!-- /ANCHOR:faq -->

title: "deep-review: Autonomous Multi-Iteration Code Review Loop"
description: "Iterative code-review skill that runs multiple review cycles with fresh context per iteration, file-based state continuity, severity-weighted convergence detection, and a binary release-readiness verdict."
trigger_phrases:

13: # Autonomous Deep Review Loop
14: 
15: Iterative code review and quality auditing protocol with fresh context per iteration, externalized state, convergence detection, and severity-weighted findings (P0/P1/P2).

23: 
24: ## 1. WHEN TO USE
25: 
26: ### When to Use This Skill
27: 
28: Use this skill when:
29: - Code quality audit requiring multiple rounds across different review dimensions
30: - Spec folder validation requiring cross-reference checks between docs and implementation
31: - Release readiness check before shipping a feature or component
32: - Finding misalignments between spec documents and actual code
33: - Verifying cross-references across documentation, agents, commands, and code
34: - Iterative review where each dimension's findings inform subsequent dimensions
35: - Unattended or overnight audit sessions
36: 
37: ### When NOT to Use
38: 
39: - Simple single-pass code review (use `sk-code-review` instead)
40: - Known issues that just need fixing (go directly to implementation)
41: - Implementation tasks (use `sk-code` or `/speckit:implement`)
42: - Quick one-file checks (use direct Grep/Read)
43: - Fewer than 2 review dimensions needed (single-pass suffices)
44: 
45: ### FORBIDDEN INVOCATION PATTERNS
46: 
47: This skill is invoked EXCLUSIVELY through the `/deep:start-review-loop` command. The command's YAML workflow owns state, dispatch, and convergence.
48: 
49: **NEVER:**
50: - Write a custom bash/shell dispatcher to parallelize iterations
51: - Invoke cli-codex / cli-gemini / cli-claude-code directly in a loop to simulate iterations
52: - Manually write iteration prompts to `/tmp` and dispatch them via `copilot -p`
53: - Dispatch the `@deep-review` LEAF agent via the Task tool for iteration loops (the agent is LEAF, a single iteration, and MUST be driven by the command's workflow)
54: - Skip the state machine: `deep-review-state.jsonl`, `deep-review-config.json`, `deltas/`, `prompts/`, `logs/`
55: - Manage iteration state outside the resolved local review packet under `{spec_folder}/review/`
56: 
57: **ALWAYS:**
58: - Invoke via `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` or `/deep:start-review-loop :confirm`
59: - Let the command's YAML workflow own dispatch (auto: `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`)
60: - Let `scripts/reduce-state.cjs` be the SINGLE state writer
61: - Require every iteration to produce BOTH the markdown narrative AND the JSONL delta (dispatch scripts must fail if either is missing)
62: - Use `resolveArtifactRoot(specFolder, 'review')` from `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs` to locate the canonical review root

2: title: "Deep Review Convergence Signals"
3: description: "Focused reference for severity-weighted stop signals, P0 overrides, quality gates, and graph-aware blocked-stop checks."
4: trigger_phrases:

10: 
11: # Deep Review Convergence Signals
12: 
13: This reference names the live stop signals. Use `references/convergence.md` as the hub when you need algorithms, examples, and sibling threshold notes.
14: 
15: ---
16: 
17: ## 1. OVERVIEW
18: 
19: Deep-review convergence is a release-readiness decision. The loop stops only when severity-weighted findings stabilize, dimension coverage is complete, quality gates pass, and no P0 override is active.
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## 2. SIGNALS
24: 
25: | Signal | Meaning | Blocks STOP When |
26: |--------|---------|------------------|
27: | Severity-weighted new findings | Weighted P0/P1/P2 discovery ratio for the latest iteration | Above the configured threshold |
28: | Rolling stop average | Stabilized trend across recent iterations | Still above the stop band |
29: | Dimension coverage | Required review dimensions and traceability overlays covered | Any required dimension is missing |
30: | Quality-gate bundle | Evidence, scope, coverage, P0, density, hotspot, claim, candidate, graphless gates | Any required gate fails |
31: | Graph-aware blind spot check | Coverage graph sees unresolved structural gaps | Graph blockers remain active |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 3. P0 OVERRIDE
36: 
37: Any new P0 forces at least one more iteration. The override exists because a new blocker changes the release verdict, even if the rest of the stop math appears stable.
38: 
39: P0 handling requires:
40: 
41: - File and line evidence.
42: - Adversarial self-check.
43: - Active registry update.
44: - Replay in synthesis before PASS is legal.
45: 
46: ---
47: 
48: ## 4. LEGAL STOP
49: 
50: STOP is legal only when all of these are true:
51: 
52: 1. New findings are below threshold.
53: 2. Rolling trend is stable.
54: 3. All required dimensions are covered.
55: 4. The quality-gate bundle passes.
56: 5. No unresolved P0 remains.
57: 6. Graph-aware blockers are absent or explicitly unavailable with fallback evidence.
58: 
59: If any item fails, the workflow emits `blocked_stop` and continues or routes to recovery.
60: 
61: ---
62: 
63: ## 5. SIBLING CONTRAST
64: 
65: Do not carry thresholds across deep skills:
66: 
67: | Skill | Primary Signal | Default Threshold |
68: |-------|----------------|-------------------|
69: | `deep-review` | Severity-weighted P0/P1/P2 new findings | `0.10` |
70: | `deep-research` | Novelty/newInfoRatio over research knowledge | `0.05` |
71: | `deep-ai-council` | Planning agreement with critique blocker check | Council-specific, not interchangeable |
72: 
73: ---
74: 
75: ## 6. RELATED RESOURCES
76: 
77: - `references/convergence.md` for the full stop contract.
78: - `references/state_outputs.md` for dashboard and report fields that display convergence.
79: - `references/state_reducer_registry.md` for reducer-owned gate state.

(End of file - total 79 lines)
</content>

11: # Deep Review State Outputs
12: 
13: This reference explains the files operators read during and after a review loop. Use `references/state_format.md` for schemas and field-level contracts.
14: 
15: ---
16: 
17: ## 1. OVERVIEW
18: 
19: Deep-review outputs are audit artifacts. Iteration agents write evidence, the reducer writes derived state, and synthesis writes the release-readiness report.
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## 2. PACKET FILES
24: 
25: | File | Owner | Mutability | Purpose |
26: |------|-------|------------|---------|
27: | `deep-review-config.json` | Workflow | Immutable after init | Run parameters, lineage, threshold settings |
28: | `deep-review-state.jsonl` | Workflow and iteration contract | Append-only | Config, iteration, blocked-stop, pause, synthesis events |
29: | `deep-review-findings-registry.json` | Reducer | Regenerated | Active, resolved, repeated, and blocked findings |
30: | `deep-review-strategy.md` | Reducer with operator-readable sections | Regenerated/updated | Dimension order, known context, next focus |
31: | `deep-review-dashboard.md` | Reducer | Regenerated | Current status, trend, verdict preview |
32: | `iterations/iteration-NNN.md` | Iteration agent | Write-once | Detailed findings for one pass |
33: | `review-report.md` | Synthesis | Final output | Findings-first release-readiness report |
34: | `resource-map.md` | Synthesis | Final or disabled | Converged delta evidence map |
35: 
36: ---
37: 
38: ## 3. ITERATION MARKDOWN
39: 
40: Each iteration file must be non-empty and end with exactly one final verdict line:
41: 
42: ```text
43: Review verdict: PASS
44: ```
45: 
46: ```text
47: Review verdict: CONDITIONAL
48: ```
49: 
50: ```text
51: Review verdict: FAIL
52: ```
53: 
54: The line maps to the findings in that iteration only. Synthesis computes the final release verdict from the full state.
55: 
56: ---
57: 
58: ## 4. DASHBOARD
59: 
60: The dashboard is a status view, not the source of truth. It should display:
61: 
62: - Current iteration and next focus.
63: - Active P0/P1/P2 counts.
64: - Dimension coverage.
65: - Convergence trend.
66: - Gate blockers.
67: - Provisional verdict.
68: 
69: If dashboard data disagrees with JSONL, JSONL wins and the reducer must regenerate the dashboard.
70: 
71: ---
72: 
73: ## 5. FINAL REPORT
74: 
75: `review-report.md` is the handoff artifact. It carries:
76: 
77: - Executive summary.
78: - Planning trigger.
79: - Active finding registry.
80: - Remediation workstreams.
81: - Spec seed.
82: - Plan seed.
83: - Traceability status.
84: - Deferred items.
85: - Audit appendix.
86: - Optional Resource Map Coverage Gate section when a map existed at init.
87: 
88: ---
89:
